methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $37M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Cellcolabs is a Series A-stage biotech company that has raised $37M to date. The valuation universe includes 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals, though narrow-set peer matching proved insufficient for direct median application.

Of three triangulation methods evaluated, only stage-progression anchoring proved applicable. Using total capital raised ($37M) as a proxy for Series A valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for progression to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $74M. The peer-median approach was not executable due to fewer than three same-stage or similar-tier comparables meeting filter criteria. Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 6.9x across the three biotech tickers could not be applied because Cellcolabs' revenue is not on file, which is typical for private-stage companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $63M to $85M, with a median of $74M. This range reflects conservative (15% discount) and optimistic (15% upside) scenarios around the stage-progression result. Primary uncertainties include the absence of granular comparable company data at the narrow peer tier and lack of revenue visibility, limiting the analytical framework to a single executable method. Deeper diligence on product-stage progress and clinical metrics would refine this estimate.
